behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git/repo state evaluation plus PR-state lookup criteria. MOCK-MISS: unit-only logic tests would miss mismatches between commit ancestry/time extraction and branch/PR state wiring. REAL-INFRA: this behavior depends on real git semantics for "commits not in main" and commit age, typically via subprocess/repo state. Rejection-gate: not Agentic/Workflow because behavior text has no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo with main+feature branches and dated commits, run the actual staleness detector path used in production (including git queries/subprocesses), provide PR state as no-open-PR input, and assert it marks stale only when all three conditions hold.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration (startup hook/session bootstrap + staleness result + warning renderer). REAL-INFRA brake: no required real network/OS primitive in the behavior text itself; stale state can be injected. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agentic pipeline: none. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified by behavior phrase focusing on emission logic at session start, not real subprocess/network correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire session bootstrap modules with a deterministic stale branch state fixture and verify warning emission includes actionable branch context (branch name/age/next action), and verify no warning when fixture is non-stale.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a "local status-classifier function" with deterministic mapping; single-function logic boundary. MOCK-MISS: module interaction is not required to trigger this failure. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified because text limits scope to local deterministic mapping, no I/O, no model.
behaviors.test_description: Call the classifier function with table-driven branch-state inputs and assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) including edge values around thresholds already encoded in input state.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says "against live repo and PR state"; mocking would hide real failures in subprocess/API integration and rendering pipeline. REAL-INFRA: requires real repo state and real external PR state call/HTTP path. Rejection-gate: not Agentic/Workflow because correctness is rule-based classification/rendering, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in an environment with a real git repo and real PR backend state for two scenarios (clean vs commits-without-PR) and assert rendered output contains the correct status text and stale marker only in the stale scenario.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff between staleness evaluator and PR-state interpretation (open/merged should suppress stale-no-pr). In-process wiring with controlled PR-state fixtures can catch real logic/integration errors. Integration-brake checks: no required real OS/network primitive in behavior statement, no AI dependency, no multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: System not required unless specifically validating transport/API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Compose detector + PR-state adapter using deterministic fixtures for open and merged PR cases and assert status is never `stale-no-pr`; include a no-PR control case to confirm contrast.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Rebase/squash semantics are real VCS history behaviors; mocks/fixtures often miss ancestry and reachability edge cases after hash rewrites. REAL-INFRA: requires actual git history transformations (rebase/squash) and re-evaluation through production detection path. Rejection-gate: not Agentic/Workflow; no model decisions in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temporary git repo, create stale-looking branch history, then perform actual rebase/squash-merge operations that change commit hashes while integrating work, run detector, and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale when history indicates work is already represented in main/PR outcome.
